frozen_components: language model (Gemini 2.5 Flash, Qwen 3.6 35B, or other frozen LLM serving as verifier)
architecture.summary: LLM-as-a-Verifier is a training-free verification framework that scores solution trajectories by computing the expectation over the distribution of scoring-token logits rather than collapsing to discrete scores. Given a task, two candidate trajectories, and evaluation criteria, the system constructs scoring prompts, extracts logprobs from a frozen language model's output, and aggregates them via Eq. 3.1 across three scaling dimensions (granularity of score tokens, repeated evaluations, criteria decomposition) to produce continuous reward signals. A cost-efficient Probabilistic Pivot Tournament algorithm then selects the highest-scoring trajectory from candidate pools. Fine-grained verifier scores are used for test-time ranking, progress monitoring, and as dense rewards for reinforcement learning.

claims.statement: LLM-as-a-Verifier achieves state-of-the-art performance on Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%)

baseline.name: Pass@1 (heterogeneous pool: Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 Flash, MiniMax M2.5)

claims.statement: LLM-as-a-Verifier achieves 87.4% preference accuracy on RoboRewardBench
Scaling scoring granularity from 1 to 20 tokens improves Signal-to-Noise Ratio from 0.775 to 0.799 and pairwise verification accuracy from baseline to higher accuracy on Terminal-Bench
Repeated evaluation scaling improves pairwise verification accuracy from 77.5% at 1 repetition to 81.2% at 5 repetitions on Terminal-Bench V2
LLM-as-a-Verifier achieves Spearman Value-Order Correlation of 0.966 on RoboRewardBench with 5 repetitions and granularity 20, substantially exceeding RoboReward-8B (0.877) and TOPReward (0.565)

LLM-as-a-Verifier can serve as a drop-in dense reward signal for both off-policy (SAC) and on-policy (GRPO) reinforcement learning, improving sample efficiency without requiring reward-model training or environment-specific shaping
